# Slash Commands

> Register and execute text commands with a / prefix in chat messages

**Category:** Integration · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** commands · chat · extensibility · shortcuts · integration

## What this does

Register and execute text commands with a / prefix in chat messages

Specifies 6 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **command** *(text, required)* — Command Name
- **params** *(text, optional)* — Command Parameters
- **room_id** *(text, required)* — Room ID
- **user_id** *(text, required)* — User ID
- **description** *(text, required)* — Description
- **params_example** *(text, optional)* — Parameters Example
- **permission** *(text, optional)* — Required Permission
- **provides_preview** *(boolean, optional)* — Provides Preview

## What must be true

- **general:** Command names must be unique across the platform; registering a duplicate command replaces or errors depending on configuration, Command names are lowercased and must contain only alphanumeric characters and hyphens, Command execution is triggered only when the full message starts with /, followed by a registered command name, Commands with a required permission are hidden from users who lack that permission, If provides_preview is true, the platform calls the previewer function as the user types and shows up to 10 preview items, Selecting a preview item triggers executePreviewItem instead of the main executor, Command callbacks receive read-only access to the platform data; mutations go through the modify accessor, Commands may send ephemeral messages back to the invoking user without posting to the room, Unrecognized command names should surface a help hint rather than silently fail, Extensions and apps may register, enable, or disable commands dynamically at runtime

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Preview Shown** — when user is typing a command whose provides_preview is true; user has not yet submitted the message, then Platform calls the previewer and displays up to 10 inline preview items to the user.
- **Preview Item Selected** — when preview was shown; user selects one of the preview items, then Platform calls executePreviewItem with the selected item instead of the main executor.
- **Command Executed** — when user sends a message starting with / followed by a registered command name; user has the required permission for the command (if any), then Command handler is invoked with the command name, params, room context, and user context.

**❌ Failure paths**

- **Command Not Found** — when user sends a message starting with / followed by a name that is not a registered command, then Platform surfaces a help message or ignores the unknown command. *(error: `SLASH_COMMAND_NOT_FOUND`)*
- **Permission Denied** — when user sends a message starting with / followed by a registered command name; command requires a permission the user does not hold, then Command is not displayed to the user and execution is rejected. *(error: `SLASH_COMMAND_PERMISSION_DENIED`)*
- **Command Disabled** — when user invokes a command that is currently disabled by an extension or admin, then Execution is blocked and an informational message may be shown to the user. *(error: `SLASH_COMMAND_DISABLED`)*

## Errors it can return

- `SLASH_COMMAND_NOT_FOUND` — The command you entered was not found. Type /help to see available commands.
- `SLASH_COMMAND_PERMISSION_DENIED` — You do not have permission to use this command.
- `SLASH_COMMAND_DISABLED` — This command is currently disabled.

## Connects to

- **bot-plugin-framework** *(recommended)* — Extensions and bots use the plugin framework to register slash commands dynamically
- **role-based-access-control** *(optional)* — Permission-gated commands rely on RBAC to determine access
